zh.lifeSummary: 當Anthony Fisher於1591年4月23日在英國素福爾可希勒哈姆受洗時，他的父親Anthony Fisher34歲，母親Mary Fiske30歲。他於大約1615年與Mrs. Alice Fisher在英國結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1671年4月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特享年80歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fisher
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle English fis(sc)her(e) ‘fisherman’ (Old English fiscere). In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from many other languages, including German Fischer and its Slavic(ized) variant Fišer (see Fiser ), Dutch Visser , Hungarian Halász (see Halasz ), Italian Pescatore , Slovenian Ribič (see Ribic ), and Croatian Ribić or Ribar .

English: in a few cases, possibly a topographic name for someone who lived near a fish weir on a river, from Middle English fis(sc)hwere, fisshyar ‘fish weir’ (Old English fiscwer, fiscgear), or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Fisher in North Mundham, Sussex.
Irish: translation into English of Gaelic Ó Bradáin ‘descendant of Bradán’, a personal name meaning ‘salmon’. See Braden .

variants.name: Fischer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Gerhard, Helmut, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Dieter, Franz, Ernst.

German, Danish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle High German visch, German Fisch ‘fish’ + the agent suffix -er. This form of the surname is also established in some other parts of Europe, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, and the Netherlands. In Poland, Czechia, Croatia, and Slovenia it is (also) established in the Slavicized forms Fiszer (Polish) and Fišer (see Fiser ). Compare Fisher .

nameMeanings.name: Anthony
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual English form of the old Roman family name Antonius, which is of uncertain (probably Etruscan) origin. The spelling with -th- (not normally reflected in the pronunciation) represents a learned but erroneous attempt to associate it with Greek anthos ‘flower’. In the post-classical period it was a common name, borne by various early saints, most notably a 3rd-century Egyptian hermit monk, who is regarded as the founder of Christian monasticism.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Anthony Fisher was christened on 23 April 1591, in Syleham, Suffolk, England, his father, Anthony Fisher, was 34 and his mother, Mary Fiske, was 30. He married Mrs. Alice Fisher about 1615, in England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. He died in April 1671, in Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anthony Fisher a été baptisé le 23 avril 1591, à Syleham, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Anthony Fisher, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Mary Fiske, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Mrs. Alice Fisher environ 1615, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en avril 1671, à Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 80 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anthony Fisher am 23. April 1591 in Syleham, Suffolk, England getauft wurde, war sein Vater Anthony Fisher 34 und seine Mutter Mary Fiske 30. ko.lifeSummary: Anthony Fisher(이)가 1591년 4월 23일 영국 수폴크 실함에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Anthony Fisher(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Fiske(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1615년 영국에서 Mrs. Alice Fisher(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1671년 4월 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 도르체스터에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Anthony Fisher foi batizado em 23 de abril de 1591, em Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Anthony Fisher, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Mary Fiske, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs. Alice Fisher aproximadamente 1615, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em abril de 1671, em Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 80 anos.

ru.Dedham, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colon: дедхам, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anthony Fisher был крещен 23 апреля 1591 г. в местности силэам, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Anthony Fisher было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Mary Fiske, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Alice Fisher около 1615 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер апрель 1671 г. в местности дорчестир, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 80 лет.
